history.text:
Street Paving
July 15th Update
E Lothrop St
(Bisson to Hale St)
- Water work will continue early this summer from Bisson St to Hale St. Once the water work is complete the sidewalks and street will be repaved.
Laurel/Walnut
- The reclaim contractor is scheduled to drop the structures on Monday July 27th and start reclaim on Wednesday July 29th.
Independence Park
- Construction continues on the improvements at the park.
Sargent/Wilson/Lincoln/Fillmore
- Handicap ramp work continues. Sidewalk on Lincoln St will commence this week.
Lexington Dr -
The paving contractor will grind the cul-de-sac tomorrow and will schedule the new pavement ASAP.
Cabot St
-
The area in front of the middle school will be milled tomorrow and final pavement will be scheduled ASAP. The handicap ramps will be rebuilt starting next week.
During sidewalk construction, it is possible that trees may need to be remove and eventually replaced where possible. Due to the nature of the work, unmarked irrigation systems or buried invisible dog fences within the City's right-of-way may become accidentally damaged. If you have either of each, close to the edge of the road/back of the sidewalk, please mark the locations. The City and its Contractor are not responsible for irrigation systems or buried invisible dog fences in the City's right-of-way but will make every effort to not damage them. Please check this website frequently for Paving Related Updates. Follow us on
Twitter @BeverlyEngineer
.
In 2024, the City began to implement the 3-year paving plan. This plan is subject to change at any time due to shifting priorities and/or available funding. We currently have programmed the following streets for the 2025 and 2026 construction seasons. Timing of these streets is subject to change at any time.

Important Information for Those Residents Affected by the Street Paving Projects
Upon completion of the paving projects, the newly paved streets will not be permitted to be dug/opened for at least five years based on the
road age and/or Pavement Condition Index (PCI) rating (PDF)
(if approved by the Engineering Department). Therefore, if there is any pending underground utility work to be performed it must be completed prior to completion of the paving project.
Examples of Underground Utility work are but not limited to:
Cable
Electric
Natural Gas
: In the event you do not have natural gas service and are interested in changing to natural gas, we would suggest that you contact National Grid (800-233-5325) to schedule your service at this time. Please notify the Engineering Department if you need assistance you with expediting your request 978-605-2358).
Sewer
(chronic issues should be addressed at this time and should be brought to the attention of the Engineering Department 978-605-2358)
Sump Pump
: If you have a sump pump it may be possible to connect to the City's drainage infrastructure. Call the Engineering Department if you need assistance with this matter 978-605-2358).
Water
(volume, pressure, and quality issues should be brought to the attention of the Water Department at this time 978-921-6057)
The Engineering Department is available to assist you with any questions or concerns you have. We will also be updating this important project with information that can be found on this link as it becomes available.
Beverly's Road Pavement Condition Report
PCI ratings report
.
